english: signification of gtan-du (gtan du) e.g. to cut off, to deliver completely; gtán-nas (gtan nas) id.; gtan-krigs (gtan krigs) agreement, stipulation, convention, gtan-krigs byéd-pa (gtan krigs byed pa) Mil. Note. Owing to its second signification gtan (gtan) is often confounded with brtan(-po) (brtan po), or even with bstan(-pa) (bstan pa). Not only illiterate people, but well-educated Lamas from C. were occasionally doubtful as to the correct spelling of this word.

(Ssk. हेतु, Stg., Do, f. 344 gtan-tsigs-kyi de-kho-na-nyíd bstán-pa (gtan tshigs kyi de kho na nyid bstan pa) = हेतुतत्त्वउपदेश) 1. argument, syllogism Cs.; evidence before a court of justice Dzl. MS, 6. — 2. Sch.: a standing proposition, indisputable point Thgy. (where in my Ms. brtan-tsigs (brtan tshigs) is erron. written; v. the note to the preceding word). — 3. logic, dialectics Cs.; gtan-tsigs-méd-par smrá-ba (gtan tshigs med par smra ba) is in Stg. the term applied to a kind of kyál-ka (kyal ka), evidently: illogical, irrational talk; gtan-tsigs-su bzéd-nas (gtan tshigs su bzed nas) Glr. 96. wishing to clear up, to render evident (?); gtan-tsigs-mkhan (gtan tshigs mkhan) dialectician, logician.

(कथा) talk, discourse, speech. 1. in a general sense: *tam čig-pa* (*tam cig pa*) C., *tam čig-čig* (*tam cig cig*) W., that is one and the same talk, that means the same; gtam bsdúr-ba (gtam bsdur ba) to compare depositions, to examine, to try judicially, *tam-dúr* (*tam dur*) W. trial, judicial examination. — 2. news, tidings, intelligence, gtám bzáṅ-po (gtam bzang po) good news; phýis gtam mi ḥdúg (phyis gtam mi 'dug) after which there are no further accounts Mil.; *tam śád-če* (*tam shad ce*) to tell a tale, a story W.; report, rumour, fame, de phúl zér-baí gtam rgyál-pos thós-nas (de phul zer ba'i gtam rgyal pos thos nas) when the king heard the report that ... had been delivered up Pth.; thag-ríṅ-gi gtam (thag ring gi gtam) fame of remote matters or events; bdág-gis gtám-du thós-na (bdag gis gtam du thos na) as I have learned, have been told Dzl. — 3. section, chapter Tar., frq. Phrases. gtam gléṅ-ba (gtam gleng ba) S.O., Dzl., gtám-du gléṅ-ba (gtam du gleng ba) Dzl. to speak, to converse, to discourse; gtam byéd-pa (gtam byed pa), smrá-ba (smra ba), zér-ba (zer ba) id.; gtam byar ḥgróo (gtam byar 'gro'o) I shall go and speak to him Dzl.; the genit. preceding gtam (gtam) always denotes the person or thing spoken of, not the person speaking; čí-ltar gyúr-paí gtam byas (ci ltar gyur pa'i gtam byas) he gave an account of the manner how it had happened Dzl.; mthún-paí gtam byéd-pa (mthun pa'i gtam byed pa) to negotiate about peace Glr.; čhós(-kyi) gtam byéd-pa (chos (kyi) gtam byed pa) to begin a religious conversation Mil.; ṅa dé-ltar byéd-paí gtam mí-la ma lab (nga de ltar byed pa'i gtam mi la ma lab) do not tell any body that I am doing this Mil.; in a similar manner: mi rúṅ-baí gtam bsgrág-go (mi rung ba'i gtam bsgrag go) he shall declare it to be unbecoming Thgr.; pha-máí gtam dris (pha ma'i gtam dris) he inquired about his parents Dzl.; bú-moí gtam thos (bu mo'i gtam thos) he heard of the girl Dzl. Comp. gtam-rgyúd (gtam rgyud) tradition, oral account; deí gtam-rgyúd (de'i gtam rgyud) the legend of him. — gtam-ṅán (gtam ngan) ill report, slander. — gtam-snyán (gtam snyan) joyful news, glad tidings, sgróg-pa (sgrog pa) to announce Mil. — gtam-bsdúr (gtam bsdur) v. above. — gtám-dpe (gtam dpe) a proverb, a saying Cs. — gtam-rtsúb (gtam rtsub) rough speech, abusive language. Note. In W. *(s)pé-ra* (*(s)pe ra*) is more in use than *tam* (*tam*).

1. adj full, spú-gri gtám(s)-pa (spu gri gtam(s) pa) quite full of razors Thgy.; also Lexx.; more frq. it is spelled (b)ltám(s)-pa ((b)ltam(s) pa). — 2. vb. to appoint, to commission, of rare occurrence. — 3. sbst. Cs.: a term for a thousand billion, yet v. the remark to dkrígs-pa (dkrigs pa).

Lexx. (cf. gté-pa (gte pa)) pawn, pledge, gtá-mar ḥjúg-pa (gta' mar 'jug pa) to pawn, to give as a pledge, gtá-ma blú-ba (gta' ma blu ba) to redeem a pledge Cs; *nór-ta* (*nor ta*) W. jewels, precious stones, given as a pledge (Cs.: 'pecuniary security, bail'); mí-gta (mi gta') a hostage Cs.
with khrag (khrag), to bleed, to let blood Med.; gtár(-bar) byéd-pa (gtar (bar) byed pa), rtsá-ba-la (rtsa ba la) from a vein, or also gtár-ga ḥdébs-pa (gtar ga 'debs pa) id.

(तमस) gloom, mental darkness, ignorance, stupidity, gléṅ-pa gti-mug-čan (gleng pa gti mug can) infatuated fools Dzl.; mtsán-mo gti-mug-gnyíd-du soṅ (mtshan mo gti mug gnyid du song) at night I fell into a profound sleep Mil.; in a special
